**Stelian Badea** (born 29 July 1958), widely known as **Stelică Badea**, is a retired [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") football midfielder.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stelian_Badea&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

He was born in [Bucșani](/wiki/Buc%C8%99ani,_Giurgiu "Bucșani, Giurgiu") and his first steps in football were under the guidance of coach [Stere Popescu](/w/index.php?title=Stere_Popescu&action=edit&redlink=1 "Stere Popescu (page does not exist)"). He debuted at I.O.R. Bucharest and at twenty years he joined [FC Olt Scornicești](/wiki/FC_Olt_Scornice%C8%99ti "FC Olt Scornicești"). In 1980, he arrived in [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești"), where he soon took the left midfield position. He played ten seasons at [FC Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești"), playing 244 matches and scoring twenty goals.

## Coaching activity

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stelian_Badea&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Coaching activity")]

* 1990–1992 Dacia Pitești, 3rd League
* 1992–1993 FC Argeș Pitești, 2nd League and 1st League
* 1993–2000 Dacia Pitești, 3rd League and 2nd League with 2 promovations in 2nd League and 1st League
* 2000–2001 Cimentul Fieni, 2nd League
* 2001–2004 Internațional Pitești, 2nd League
* 2004–2005 Astra Ploiești, 2nd League
* 2005–2006 FC Național II, 2nd League
* 2006–2007 AL Hilal Riyadh, Saudi Arabia
* 2010–2011 AL Riyadh, Saudi Arabia
